global_objective: Reach the first compiler completion stage
name: lsc1
rules: Each agent keeps a distinct responsibility track.
For every turn, each agent maintains current implementation, next-slot complete plan, and plus-two lookahead.
Compile or verification failures enter an attempt loop for the same slot.
A slot advances only after every active agent has an accepted patch or is explicitly marked skipped.
JSON landing records replay history; lane propagation belongs to audited amalgamation.

ed.lookahead: Turn accelerator state into the normal coordination artifact used at the start and end of each implementation turn.
ed.notes: This track should avoid compiler semantics except where tooling verification requires awareness of compiler smoke profiles.
ed.patch_goal: Fix long-argument forwarding in forks.bat and commit the initial accelerator state so other agents can consume the staged plan.
ed.plan: Submit the wrapper/state patch, verify Python tooling still compiles, then use audited gadget amalgamation to propagate the accelerator scaffold and state to the agent lanes.
ed.responsibility: Forks/gadget/accelerator operational tooling: preserve replay evidence, make lane propagation explicit, and keep the operator workflow auditable.

edd.lookahead: Prepare the next slice of admitted compiler surface after boolean logic, with expected/got diagnostics and fixture-level evidence.
edd.notes: Avoid expanding backend parity work except by defining the semantic contract backends must consume.
edd.patch_goal: Consolidate front-end behaviour around recently admitted boolean/unary constructs and improve parse/check diagnostic informativeness without broad backend ownership.
edd.plan: Read current parser, AST, checker, examples, and smoke assertions. Submit a focused patch only where front-end/checker behaviour or diagnostic text is directly implicated.
edd.responsibility: Compiler front-end and checking surface: syntax, AST, type checking, and diagnostic quality for admitted Core-1 language features.

eddy.lookahead: Prepare backend coverage for the next compiler-surface slice selected by the accelerator, with clear unsupported cases where required.
eddy.notes: Use tests to expose backend contract failures, but avoid redefining the language surface independently.
eddy.patch_goal: Keep backend output aligned with the admitted Core-1 surface and ensure smoke/snapshot tests prove the parity contract.
eddy.plan: Read current backend emitters and smoke fixtures. Submit a focused backend/test patch for any remaining parity or error-surface gap without taking parser/checker ownership.
eddy.responsibility: Backend parity and test evidence: TypeScript/Haskell emission, unsupported-expression failures, smoke tests, and snapshots.

slots.objective: Stabilise the first compiler completion surface, preserve operational evidence, and establish non-overlapping agent lanes.

ed.lookahead: Prepare an operator-level command that prints the full C1 lane health packet.
ed.plan: Extend accelerator reporting so each slot can summarise accepted replay entries and outstanding lanes before advance.
ed.responsibility: Operational acceptance gate and replay/amalgamation evidence.

edd.lookahead: Identify the next admitted expression/function construct requiring parser/checker work.
edd.plan: Define and implement the next narrow diagnostic improvement for parse/check errors, keeping message codes and source locations stable where available.

eddy.lookahead: Identify backend snapshot or parity risks for the construct after next.
eddy.plan: Add or revise smoke/snapshot coverage for the next accepted front-end surface, keeping backend unsupported failures explicit.
eddy.responsibility: Backend acceptance criteria and evidence snapshots.

slots.objective: Move from current stabilisation into a documented C1 acceptance gate with per-agent deliverables and verification evidence.

ed.lookahead: Add accelerator affordances for path-collision review, slot packets, and accepted/skipped transition summaries.

edd.lookahead: Propose the next minimal admitted syntax/typechecking slice after current boolean and function-surface work.

eddy.lookahead: Propose the backend evidence required for the next admitted syntax/typechecking slice.

slots.objective: Prepare the next compiler completion stride after C1 stabilisation without letting agents collapse into the same subproblem.
